rationale: Authority data for W40K-0151..W40K-0160 — the **Space-Wolves-original cluster**: one trilogy-omnibus opener (W40K-0151 *The Inquisition War Omnibus* aggregating the three Watson novels just authored in ssot-w40k-015) followed by the **Ragnar Blackmane saga** as William-King-launched it in 1999-2003 (*Space Wolf* + *Ragnar's Claw* + *Grey Hunter* + *Wolfblade*) and as **Lee Lightner** continued it 2007-2008 (*Sons of Fenris* + *Wolf's Honour*), two Space-Wolf-aggregation-omnibuses (W40K-0158 *The Space Wolf Omnibus* collecting King's first trilogy + W40K-0159 *Space Wolf: The Second Omnibus* collecting King's fourth-volume + the Lightner pair), and one **2025-publication-frontier entry** (W40K-0160 *Krakenblood* by Marc Collins — Wolf-Priest Ivar Krakenblood as a fresh Space-Wolves-protagonist outside the Ragnar-saga register, sixth Marc-Collins Black-Library novel pivoting from Emperor's Children to Space Wolves). **First authority-layer Space-Wolves-as-primary-protagonist-faction cluster**; prior authority-layer Space-Wolves appearances were peripheral (Logan Grimnar mentioned, Russ mentioned, generic Astartes-Space-Wolves-faction-tier) — this batch establishes the chapter as a primary on-page register across **nine** of ten entries (every entry except W40K-0151 itself, which is the Inquisition-War-omnibus). **Three omnibuses in one batch** (W40K-0151 + W40K-0158 + W40K-0159) — the highest omnibus-density of any authority-layer batch so far (prior peak was ssot-w40k-015 with one *Green Tide* omnibus); aggregations per Brief-061 constraint pull factions/locations/characters/facetIds from constituent novels rather than the framing material, and the constituent-content is authority-layer-internal-aggregation from this and the prior batch (W40K-0151 aggregates W40K-0148+0149+0150 from ssot-w40k-015; W40K-0158 aggregates W40K-0152+0153+0154 from this batch; W40K-0159 aggregates W40K-0155+0156+0157 from this batch). **First-authority-layer-as-primary-protagonist surface forms**: Ragnar Blackmane (Ragnar Thunderfist) + Strybjorn Grimskull + Sven Brujothirson + Berek Thunderfist + Wolf-Priest Ranek + Inquisitor Ivan Sternberg + Lady Gabriela Belisarius + Torin + Madox the Thousand-Sons-Sorcerer + Ivar Krakenblood + Ulrik the Slayer as first-authority-layer character surface forms; Fenris + The Fang + Asaheim + Aerius + Galt + Venam + Garm + Terra + Hyades + the Spear of Russ + the Talisman of Lykos + the Russvik tribe + the Thunderfist tribe + the Grimskull tribe + House Belisarius + Wolfblade as first-authority-layer location / faction / artefact surface forms. **`skip-50-stop` marker explicit**: Maintainer-Marker `skip-50-stop` aktiv im Eröffnungs-Prompt (literal substring + German-context-elaboration `Pre-Check übergehen, Override produzieren`); Pre-Check would have fired the natural Resolver-Pause-Schwelle at `cumulativeBefore=150 % 50 == 0` (the third 50er-pause-window since loop-start), but per Brief-071-Vertrag (Acceptance-Bullet auf Brief-061-Zeile 131 als authoritative-Reading) the success-path commits Override + Status-Log together when skip is active, and Brief-061-Constraint-Note auf Zeile 79 ('CC produziert in diesem Fall KEINE neue Override-Datei, KEINEN Status-Log-Eintrag') is explicitly überschrieben für diesen Driver-Lauf. Brief-071-Vertrag-Single-Commit-Discipline: Override-Datei + Status-Log-Append in einem Commit ohne Co-Author-Trailer, keine sonstigen Datei-Edits. **WebSearch: mean ≈ 0.7 / book, max = 1 (over 10 books), 7 total searches** — one obligatory plot-confirmation search per entry for the older 1999-2008 Space-Wolves-novels (W40K-0152 *Space Wolf*, W40K-0153 *Ragnar's Claw*, W40K-0154 *Grey Hunter*, W40K-0155 *Wolfblade*, W40K-0156 *Sons of Fenris*, W40K-0157 *Wolf's Honour*) + one obligatory plot-confirmation search for W40K-0160 *Krakenblood* (2025-publication-frontier, no training-data coverage). Zero searches for the three omnibuses (W40K-0151 + W40K-0158 + W40K-0159 — pure aggregations of just-confirmed-constituent-content) and zero for the W40K-0151 entry whose constituent content was just authored in ssot-w40k-015 last iteration. **Resolver-Pre-Note**: `cumulativeAfter=160`; **next natural Resolver-Pause-Schwelle ist bei `cumulativeBefore=200`** (`ssot-w40k-021`, W40K-0201..W40K-0210). Surface-form-pile since the last Resolver-Pass in 069 spans ssot-w40k-006..016 = 110 Bücher — the resolver-deferred pile is now larger than the prior post-069 pile was at the second resolver-trigger-window; if Cowork does not write the Resolver-Brief before cumulative=200, the third natural Resolver-Pause will fire there. **Brief-071-Vertrag aktiv**: Override-Datei + Status-Log-Append in einem Commit ohne Co-Author-Trailer, keine sonstigen Datei-Edits.

overrides.synopsis: Jaq Draco's Inquisition War is gathered here as one feverish early-40K epic: an Ordo Malleus inquisitor sent to cleanse a Genestealer-tainted hive world instead uncovers the Hydra, a rogue cabal convinced it can solve the fate of humanity's soul. His pursuit carries him from Stalinvast and a Harlequin rite of catastrophe to the Webway and Sabulorb, with Meh'Lindi, Vitali Googol and Grimm dragged through conspiracies that mix Inquisition paranoia, Eldar mystery and Thousand Sons sorcery. It reads like a relic from the setting's stranger youth - cosmic, heretical, overripe and more interested in forbidden metaphysics than clean war-story closure.

On Fenris, Ragnar of the Thunderfist tribe is left dying after Strybjorn Grimskull destroys his family and his world. The Space Wolves take both enemies to the Fang, where gene-seed trials, Blood Claw brutality and Wolf Priest Ranek's discipline turn tribal fury into Chapter loyalty. Ragnar's hatred of Strybjorn has to survive pack-bond, war and the Curse of the Wulfen, especially when his friend Kjel falls to the beast within. A harsh coming-of-age saga of ice, blood-feud and the making of Ragnar Blackmane.

Inquisitor Ivan Sternberg comes to the Fang seeking Space Wolves help to recover the Talisman of Lykos, an Imperial relic he believes can halt the Chaos corruption consuming Aerius. Ragnar Blackmane, Strybjorn Grimskull and Sven Brujothirson escort the mission from the jungles of Galt to Venam's poisoned hives and into the heart of the enemy. The quest turns the old Fenrisian blood-feud into battlefield brotherhood, with Berek Thunderfist and Ranek shaping Ragnar's first wider war. A relic-hunt war-story about loyalty earned under fire.

On the corrupted world of Garm, Madox of the Thousand Sons has stolen the Spear of Russ and means to use it as the key to a ritual gate for Magnus the Red. Ragnar Blackmane, Strybjorn and Sven race into the old Space Wolves and Thousand Sons hatred, fighting through sorcery, relic-worship and the weight of their Chapter's history. Ragnar's choice at the portal costs the Wolves their sacred spear but saves them from a worse return, marking his passage from Blood Claw toward Grey Hunter. A brutal chapter-myth of sacrifice and inherited war.

After Garm, Ragnar Blackmane is sent far from Fenris to ancient Terra as one of the Wolfblade, the Space Wolves honour-guard sworn to House Belisarius of the Navis Nobilite. Guarding Lady Gabriela Belisarius should be a duty of oath and tradition; instead Ragnar lands in a succession crisis around the dying Paternoval Envoy, with Navigator houses scheming, assassins moving and Chaos pressure leaking through Imperial politics. The saga shifts from saga-fire to court-intrigue: a Fenrisian hunter learning how lethal civilization can be.

Reports of Chaos activity on Hyades draw Ragnar Blackmane and the Space Wolves into a campaign already shadowed by Dark Angels secrecy. What should be a purge becomes an armed trust-crisis between two proud Chapters, each reading insult and ancient grievance into the other's every move while the true heretic enemy profits from the fracture. The old Russ-and-Lion rivalry threatens to drown the battlefield before Ragnar can force anyone to look at the real war. A straight-edged action novel built around brotherhood curdling into suspicion.

The Thousand Sons come for the Space Wolves from every side, driven by Madox and the long echo of the Spear of Russ that Ragnar cast into the warp on Garm. With the Chapter under multi-front pressure, Ragnar Blackmane and his brothers turn from defence to a lightning strike against the enemy's own base, gambling that one decisive blow can end the sorcerer's tempo. The novel closes the original Ragnar saga by binding relic, rivalry and pack-loyalty into a last Space Wolves reckoning. A hard war-story about paying for old heroic choices.

The opening Ragnar Blackmane trilogy follows a Fenrisian tribesman from mortal feud to Space Wolves legend. Taken to the Fang after Strybjorn Grimskull's attack leaves him dying, Ragnar survives the Chapter's trials under Ranek, learns the savage discipline of the Blood Claws, loses Kjel to the Curse of the Wulfen, hunts the Talisman of Lykos for Inquisitor Ivan Sternberg across Aerius, Galt and Venam, and finally faces Madox of the Thousand Sons over the Spear of Russ on Garm. As an omnibus, it is the cleanest entry into Ragnar's first arc: coming-of-age, relic quest and Chapter myth fused into one winter-blooded saga.

The second Ragnar Blackmane omnibus carries the saga from Fenrisian war-story into Imperial politics and back to Chapter survival. Wolfblade sends Ragnar to Terra as sworn guard to House Belisarius, where Lady Gabriela, Torin and the Paternoval Envoy succession drag him into Navigator-house intrigue. Sons of Fenris throws him into the Hyades campaign, where Dark Angels secrecy nearly turns Chaos-hunting into inter-Chapter war. Wolf's Honour closes the arc with Madox, the Thousand Sons and the Spear of Russ. It is the back-half collection: oath-bound service, suspicion between allies and the cost of becoming a saga-hero.

Ivar Krakenblood, a Fenris-born Wolf Priest living in Ulrik the Slayer's shadow, is sent to recover a lost relic of the Space Wolves. The quest gives him the saga he craves: old comrades, untested allies, void-borne violence and the chance to prove that destiny can be seized by the throat. But Ivar's heroic hunger has teeth, and the same pride that makes him fearless can make him dangerous to the warriors who follow him. A fresh Space Wolves adventure outside the Ragnar line, full of boast, frost, faith and self-destructive glory.
